Headphones (single output).description: Simplest possible setup. Routes your default input (microphone) to your default output (speakers or headphones) so you can hear yourself or any system sound through the speakers. No virtual sinks, no extras. Use this if you don't know what profiles do or you just need 'make my mic and speakers work together'.

Per-application routing (4 channels).description: Creates three separate virtual sinks so you can route different apps to different places. 'main_sink' routes to your default output (speakers or headphones). 'capture_sink' is a loopback source — apps that record (OBS, ffmpeg, etc.) can grab audio from this source independently. 'monitor_sink' is for app monitoring — route an app here and hear it without affecting other apps. Adjust the routing in the Manage tab to point these at your preferred hardware outputs.

devices.name: capture_sink
devices.description: Capture source for OBS / recording apps

devices.name: monitor_sink
devices.description: App monitoring — hear an app without routing it elsewhere

Stereo split (apps + voice).description: Two virtual sinks: one for application audio (games, music, browser) and one for voice chat (Discord, Mumble, etc). Both are routed to your default output. This is the standard 'OBS-friendly' layout — your recorder can capture from each sink independently with no extra setup. If your voice chat and main audio need different volumes, set them per-app using the volume sliders on the Manage tab.
